baseline_summary.current_use: task design, data-contract validation, case studies, and baseline comparison
baseline_summary.split: chronological single-episode split for public-sample diagnostics

directions.current_readout: The sample supports hand trajectory forecasting and contact/object probes, but it does not yet include a full body/shape model or multi-person priors.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is a motion-energy proxy, not a SMPL/MANO body model or a generative motion prior.
extension_tasks.name: Body and Hand Motion Intensity

directions.focus: Human/hand/body motion, deformation priors, human-object interaction, affordance modeling.
directions.name: Human Modeling & Motion Understanding
next_steps: Add SMPL/SMPL-X or MANO-style body/hand parameter targets where available.
Train sequence models over multi-episode motion trajectories instead of isolated windows.
Evaluate affordance prediction on held-out objects and held-out episodes.
directions.preferred_background: Human pose/shape estimation, SMPL-style models, motion capture, or motion generation.

tasks.case_study: In the coffee-making sample, if the 20-frame window is during a pouring moment, the task asks the model to output an action such as Pour coffee or Pour milk into coffee.
tasks.current_limit: Chronological single-episode split creates unseen future action classes.
tasks.input: One 20-frame window represented by the current 8,378-d feature vector: video/depth summaries, pose, SLAM/camera pose, motion capture, IMU, calibration, and language-derived context.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> action label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Reads egocentric sensor state as the current human action; also provides a weak human-motion readout.

tasks.case_study: When the hand is moving toward a cup or bottle, the model predicts the future 3D hand-joint path.
tasks.current_limit: Forecasting is window-level and not yet a full sequence or policy model.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future mocap target -> regression head
tasks.why: Directly predicts human hand motion and supports hand-object interaction modeling.

tasks.case_study: During manipulation, the hand may touch a cup, table, or bottle. The task asks whether any contact is happening.
tasks.current_limit: The public sample is degenerate for this target because one class dominates.
tasks.input: Non-contact and non-caption feature blocks, so the answer is not directly leaked from the target labels.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: feature filter -> contact target -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Targets physical interaction state, a core affordance and manipulation signal.

tasks.case_study: If the person is pouring milk into coffee, relevant objects may include milk, cup, coffee, or container-like items.
tasks.current_limit: Object labels are language-derived and sparse in one episode.
tasks.input: Non-caption feature blocks, so the model must infer objects from sensors rather than copying the caption words.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: object vocabulary -> multi-hot labels -> sigmoid heads
tasks.why: Connects egocentric activity to manipulated objects and early object-centric state.

directions.current_readout: The current suite checks cross-modal alignment and depth/video reconstruction proxies; it does not yet train a renderer or reconstruct geometry.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This checks calibrated multi-view signal, but it is still feature retrieval, not NeRF, Gaussian Splatting, or novel-view synthesis.
extension_tasks.name: Multi-View Consistency Retrieval

directions.focus: Multi-view dynamic scene reconstruction, NeRF/Gaussian Splatting, novel-view synthesis.
directions.name: 3D/4D Reconstruction & Neural Rendering
next_steps: Use calibrated multi-view video plus SLAM pose to build per-episode camera trajectories.
Add depth-supervised point clouds, TSDF, Gaussian Splatting, or NeRF baselines.
Evaluate novel-view synthesis and temporal consistency across held-out views/time.
directions.preferred_background: 3D reconstruction, neural rendering, camera calibration, and bundle adjustment.

tasks.case_study: Use motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals from a pouring moment to retrieve the matching depth/video representation for that same moment.
tasks.current_limit: Retrieval shows an alignment signal, not geometric reconstruction.
tasks.input: Query side: motion, IMU, and camera/pose features. Candidate side: depth and video features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: modality split -> projection -> nearest-neighbor ranker
tasks.why: Tests whether synchronized modalities identify the same 4D moment, a prerequisite for reconstruction and world modeling.

tasks.case_study: Given motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals while the hand moves, predict the matching depth/video feature vector.
tasks.current_limit: Feature-vector reconstruction is not pixel, depth-map, mesh, NeRF, or Gaussian reconstruction.
tasks.input: Motion, IMU, and camera/pose features as input; depth/video features as the regression target.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: source-target split -> scaler -> regression head
tasks.why: Predicts visual/depth state from non-target sensors as a weak reconstruction/world-model objective.

tasks.case_study: Motion from a pouring moment is paired with video/depth from several windows later. The task asks the model to detect that mismatch.
tasks.current_limit: Synthetic shifts diagnose alignment but do not solve calibration or mapping.
tasks.input: A motion-side feature group and a visual/depth-side feature group, either aligned or artificially shifted.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: aligned/shifted pairs -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Detects temporal desynchronization, a key data-quality gate for multimodal reconstruction and world models.

directions.current_readout: Most of the 12 tasks directly target egocentric action, task state, interaction, grounding, and alignment.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is an action-structure probe inside one episode, not a general intent model across homes, people, or tasks.
extension_tasks.name: Action Phase Progress Estimation

directions.focus: Egocentric action and intention understanding, hand-object interaction, gaze/attention modeling, task structure modeling.
directions.name: Egocentric Vision & Interaction
next_steps: Move from single-episode chronological splits to held-out-episode splits.
Add audio features and stronger multimodal backbones for action, intent, and grounding.
Evaluate long-horizon task success prediction and action-conditioned generation.
directions.preferred_background: Video understanding, action recognition, or egocentric vision.

tasks.case_study: In the coffee-making sample, if the 20-frame window is during a pouring moment, the task asks the model to output an action such as Pour coffee or Pour milk into coffee.
tasks.current_limit: Chronological single-episode split creates unseen future action classes.
tasks.input: One 20-frame window represented by the current 8,378-d feature vector: video/depth summaries, pose, SLAM/camera pose, motion capture, IMU, calibration, and language-derived context.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> action label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Reads egocentric sensor state as the current human action; also provides a weak human-motion readout.

tasks.case_study: A pouring action may belong to a broader subtask such as preparing or pouring a drink. The model predicts that broader stage instead of a fine action.
tasks.current_limit: Single-episode ordering makes future subtasks hard to generalize.
tasks.input: The same all-modality 8,378-d window vector used by action recognition.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: window features -> subtask label builder -> classifier
tasks.why: Segments egocentric task state and provides a first proxy for symbolic world/task state.

tasks.case_study: When the demonstrator changes from preparing to pouring, the model should flag a boundary instead of a steady action window.
tasks.current_limit: Boundary class is sparse, so accuracy alone is misleading.
tasks.input: One all-modality window vector plus labels derived from action-change timestamps.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: action changes -> boundary labels -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Localizes egocentric task boundaries and diagnoses temporal state changes.

tasks.case_study: If a window shows the person preparing to pour, the target can be the action 20 frames later, such as the start of pouring.
tasks.current_limit: Unseen future labels dominate the single-episode chronological test.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future label shift -> classifier
tasks.why: Tests action intention/task-flow prediction from egocentric context.

tasks.case_study: When the hand is moving toward a cup or bottle, the model predicts the future 3D hand-joint path.
tasks.current_limit: Forecasting is window-level and not yet a full sequence or policy model.
tasks.input: The current all-modality window vector at time t.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: current features -> future mocap target -> regression head
tasks.why: Directly predicts human hand motion and supports hand-object interaction modeling.

tasks.case_study: During manipulation, the hand may touch a cup, table, or bottle. The task asks whether any contact is happening.
tasks.current_limit: The public sample is degenerate for this target because one class dominates.
tasks.input: Non-contact and non-caption feature blocks, so the answer is not directly leaked from the target labels.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: feature filter -> contact target -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Targets physical interaction state, a core affordance and manipulation signal.

tasks.case_study: If the person is pouring milk into coffee, relevant objects may include milk, cup, coffee, or container-like items.
tasks.current_limit: Object labels are language-derived and sparse in one episode.
tasks.input: Non-caption feature blocks, so the model must infer objects from sensors rather than copying the caption words.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: object vocabulary -> multi-hot labels -> sigmoid heads
tasks.why: Connects egocentric activity to manipulated objects and early object-centric state.

tasks.case_study: A query like Pour milk into coffee should rank the windows from the actual pouring moment higher than unrelated windows.
tasks.current_limit: Bag-of-objects language features are too weak for rich grounding.
tasks.input: Caption/object/interaction query features and a set of candidate sensor-window features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: query features -> candidate index -> cosine ranker
tasks.why: Grounds language annotation into egocentric sensor time and task state.

tasks.case_study: Use motion, IMU, and camera-pose signals from a pouring moment to retrieve the matching depth/video representation for that same moment.
tasks.current_limit: Retrieval shows an alignment signal, not geometric reconstruction.
tasks.input: Query side: motion, IMU, and camera/pose features. Candidate side: depth and video features.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: modality split -> projection -> nearest-neighbor ranker
tasks.why: Tests whether synchronized modalities identify the same 4D moment, a prerequisite for reconstruction and world modeling.

tasks.case_study: If window A shows reaching and window B shows pouring, the model should distinguish A then B from B then A.
tasks.current_limit: Only local adjacent ordering, not long-horizon causal modeling.
tasks.input: A pair of adjacent window vectors, plus their difference vector.
tasks.input_short: two adjacent windows plus difference vector
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: pair builder -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Checks whether features encode local time direction and task progression.

tasks.case_study: Motion from a pouring moment is paired with video/depth from several windows later. The task asks the model to detect that mismatch.
tasks.current_limit: Synthetic shifts diagnose alignment but do not solve calibration or mapping.
tasks.input: A motion-side feature group and a visual/depth-side feature group, either aligned or artificially shifted.
tasks.module_summary: input window -> feature/target builder -> baseline head -> evaluator -> artifact files
tasks.process_short: aligned/shifted pairs -> feature combiner -> binary classifier
tasks.why: Detects temporal desynchronization, a key data-quality gate for multimodal reconstruction and world models.

directions.current_readout: The current tasks probe temporal structure, object relevance, cross-modal retrieval, and modality prediction, but they do not yet build persistent maps or scene graphs.

extension_tasks.current_limit: This is a compact world-model proxy; it does not build a persistent map, scene graph, or object permanence model.
extension_tasks.name: Short-Horizon Ego-Motion Forecasting

directions.focus: Long-term consistent 3D/4D scene mapping, scene graphs, object- and space-centric representations, spatial reasoning.
directions.name: Scene Reconstruction & World Modeling
next_steps: Convert windows into persistent object/scene-state nodes with timestamps and camera poses.
Add map consistency, object permanence, and spatial relation prediction tasks.
Train held-out-episode world models that predict future observations and task state.
directions.preferred_background: Large-scale mapping, semantic reconstruction, or agent world models.

omni_plan.first_pilot: 32 held-out-episode pilot after valid episodes are staged

phases.entry_condition: One public Xperience-10M sample episode is available.
phases.name: Public-Sample Task Lab
phases.reader_takeaway: The public sample supports task design, feature contracts, walkthroughs, and baseline comparisons.

phases.entry_condition: Gated dataset access and enough storage for selected episodes.
phases.name: Multi-Episode Data Staging
phases.reader_takeaway: The next scale decision is data staging, with train/test separation at the episode level.

phases.entry_condition: At least 32 valid episodes are staged locally with no train/test episode leakage.
phases.name: 32-Episode Qwen3-Omni LoRA Pilot
phases.reader_takeaway: The first omni-model pilot should establish a complete held-out-episode training and evaluation loop.

phases.entry_condition: The 32-episode pilot trains and evaluates cleanly.
phases.name: 64-128 Episode Robustness Run
phases.reader_takeaway: The robustness run tests whether the pilot conclusions survive broader sessions and missing modalities.

phases.entry_condition: Enough multi-episode data and compute budget for larger multimodal objectives.
phases.name: Foundation and World-Model Extensions
phases.reader_takeaway: The long-term direction is richer multimodal representation learning for embodied-AI reasoning.

scale_up.access_status: Hugging Face returns 403 pending review for the full Xperience-10M gated dataset.

scope.warning: These walkthroughs explain task contracts on one public sample episode; cross-episode performance requires held-out episodes.
